questionText: Find the exact values of (a) $\sin\!\left(\arccos\tfrac{3}{5}\right)$ and (b) $\cos\!\left(\arctan\tfrac{5}{12}\right)$, justifying the sign of each answer.

steps.description: Recall what arccos and arctan mean
steps.explanation: The inverse cosine returns the unique angle in $[0,\pi]$ whose cosine is $\tfrac35$. Naming that angle $\alpha$ turns the composition into an ordinary trigonometry problem.
We want $\sin\!\big(\arccos\tfrac35\big)=\sin\alpha$, so the task reduces to finding $\sin\alpha$ from the known cosine.

steps.description: Fix the sign of sin alpha
steps.explanation: Since $\cos\alpha$ is positive and $\alpha$ lies in $[0,\pi]$, the angle is in the first quadrant, where sine is non-negative. This is why we will take the positive root.
Rearranging $\sin^2\alpha+\cos^2\alpha\equiv1$ gives $\sin^2\alpha$ directly from the known cosine.
The sign reasoning in Step 3 tells us to keep the positive root, giving $\sin\alpha=\tfrac45$.
This is the exact value of the first composition; the $3$-$4$-$5$ triangle is visible in the working.
Inverse tangent returns the unique angle in $\left(-\tfrac\pi2,\tfrac\pi2\right)$ with the given tangent. We want $\cos\beta$.

steps.description: Fix the sign of cos beta
steps.explanation: A positive tangent in the principal range forces $\beta$ into the first quadrant, where cosine is positive.
Because $\tan\beta=\dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}}=\dfrac{5}{12}$, a right triangle with legs $5$ and $12$ captures the angle $\beta$ exactly.
Pythagoras' theorem gives the hypotenuse; the sides form the familiar $5$-$12$-$13$ triangle.
Cosine is adjacent over hypotenuse, and it is positive as anticipated in Step 8.
This is the exact value of the second composition.
As a check we use $1+\tan^2\beta\equiv\sec^2\beta$, avoiding the triangle entirely.

steps.description: Confirm cos beta from the check
steps.explanation: Taking the positive root (cosine positive) and reciprocating agrees with the triangle result, confirming part (b).
Collecting the two results gives the final exact answers.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $\sin\!\big(\arccos\tfrac35\big)=\tfrac45$ and $\cos\!\big(\arctan\tfrac{5}{12}\big)=\tfrac{12}{13}$ (both positive because each inner angle lies in the first quadrant).
